commands.repair_reason: - [P2] No repair job is needed; the patch is review-clean and only needs normal maintainer merge gating.; Cleared: The diff only changes a CLI call-site option and tests; I found no concrete security or supply-chain concern. (sha=296d617772a70f9bbd152d2582dbbccf5666cb59)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds `validateCatalog: false` to the interactive `openclaw agents add` model-warning call, adds regression coverage for that option, and includes a few formatting-only test touchups.

PR surface: Source +1, Tests +53. Total +54 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for source-level reproduction: current main omits `validateCatalog: false`, and the helper performs uncached catalog validation unless that option is false. I did not run a live macOS/npm reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] No repair job is needed; the patch is review-clean and only needs normal maintainer merge gating.

target.title: fix #76284: [Bug]: openclaw agents add blocked — cannot add Jon/Atlas as separate agents
target.body:
## Summary

- Skip live model catalog validation during the interactive `openclaw agents add` model warning step.
- Add regression coverage that the agents add wizard passes `validateCatalog: false`, matching the setup wizard behavior.
- Keep the refreshed PR diff aligned with the latest main test cleanup changes.

Fixes openclaw/openclaw#76284.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The interactive `openclaw agents add` wizard can create a secondary agent without entering the live model catalog validation path that previously blocked the flow.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw source checkout on Linux at current PR head after merging latest `origin/main`, with isolated `HOME`, `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, and an empty bundled plugin directory.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `pnpm -s openclaw agents add` in a real pseudo-terminal, entered `Jon`, accepted the isolated workspace path, skipped model/auth setup, skipped chat channel setup, then ran `pnpm -s openclaw agents list --json` in the same isolated environment.
- **Evidence after fix:** The wizard completed with exit code 0 and printed `Agent "jon" ready.`. The follow-up `agents list --json` command exited 0 and included:

```json
{
  "id": "jon",
  "name": "Jon",
  "agentDir": ".../state/agents/jon/agent",
  "bindings": 0,
  "isDefault": false
}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The secondary agent was persisted to `openclaw.json` and appeared in `openclaw agents list --json`; the command did not hang or fail during the model warning step.
- **What was not tested:** A macOS npm-installed build was not run from this Linux source checkout. Channel setup was skipped so the proof stayed focused on the reported `agents add` agent-creation path.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `src/commands/agents.add.test.ts`

- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm check:changed`
  - Result: passed; selected `core` and `coreTests` lanes for the current five-file PR diff.
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm exec vitest run src/commands/agents.add.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.commands.config.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 11 tests passed.
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm exec vitest run src/agents/agent-bundle-mcp-runtime.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 27 tests passed.
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm exec vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/commands-acp.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.auto-reply-reply.config.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 47 tests passed.
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree pnpm exec vitest run src/tasks/task-domain-views.test.ts --reporter=verbose`
  - Result: 1 file passed, 6 tests passed.
- Real CLI proof above with `pnpm -s openclaw agents add` followed by `pnpm -s openclaw agents list --json`.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** `openclaw agents add` reused the model-warning helper with catalog validation enabled during the interactive wizard. That made agent creation depend on live catalog validation even when the user was only adding a local secondary agent. The setup wizard already avoids that dependency; this change applies the same `validateCatalog: false` behavior to the agents add wizard.
target.author: zhangguiping-xydt
